MoreQuietActionz
A modified copy of the argparse count action which counts down and updates
    the legacy quiet attribute at the same time.
    Used to unify verbosity handling.
    NF)

reportchars
charsa
show extra test summary info as specified by chars: (f)ailed, (E)rror, (s)kipped, (x)failed, (X)passed, (p)assed, (P)assed with output, (a)ll except passed (p/P), or (A)ll. (w)arnings are enabled by default (see --disable-warnings), 'N' can be used to reset the list. (default: 'fE').)
metavarr4

--full-tracez-don't cut any tracebacks (default is to cut).)

pytest_report_teststatus
WarningReporta}
Simple structure to hold warnings information captured by ``pytest_warning_recorded``.
    :ivar str message:
        User friendly message about the warning.
    :ivar str|None nodeid:
        nodeid that generated the warning (see ``get_location``).
    :ivar tuple|py.path.local fslocation:
        File system location of the source of the warning (see ``get_location``).
    )
zSReturn the more user-friendly information about the location of a warning, or None.
nodeid
fslocation

TerminalReporter.write_linec
Rewinds the terminal cursor to the beginning and writes the given line.
        :param erase:
            If True, will also add spaces until the full terminal width to ensure
            previous lines are properly erased.
        The rest of the keyword arguments are markup instructions.
        
eraseFrB

z TerminalReporter._get_main_color)
unknown_type_seenr5
)	Nr'
_determine_main_color
z&TerminalReporter._determine_main_colorc
keys
KNOWN_TYPESr?
listr
unknown_typesZ
found_typer<
z TerminalReporter._set_main_colorc
        Build the parts used in the last summary stats line.
        The summary stats line is the line shown at the end, "=== 12 passed, 2 errors in Xs===".
        This function builds a list of the "parts" that make up for the text in that line, in
        the example above it would be:
            [
                ("12 passed", {"green": True}),
                ("2 errors", {"red": True}
            ]
        That last dict for each line is a "markup dictionary", used by TerminalWriter to
        color output.
        The final color of the line is also determined by this function, and is the second
        element of the returned tuple.
        rB
&_build_collect_only_summary_stats_line
 _build_normal_summary_stats_liner
z)TerminalReporter.build_summary_stats_line)
keyr5
zRGet test/collection reports for the given status key, such as `passed` or `error`.c
